meta.title: Corder: Record what was said
meta.description: Corder records system audio and transcribes your meetings with Gemini 2.5 Flash. Speaker labels, searchable transcript, no bot in the call. Free to download for macOS 14+
meta.ogTitle: Corder. The Mac recorder with no bot in the call
meta.ogDescription: Record any meeting on macOS. Speakers labelled, transcript searchable. Google transcribes the audio and deletes it. No third participant ever joins your call

hero.headline: Record everything around you
hero.subhead: Keeps the {{Transcript}} on your Mac. No bot joins the call
hero.qualifier: Free forever. No subscription, no credit card

audienceLine.text: For founders taking investor calls, consultants on client kickoffs, and anyone who thinks out loud and needs it written down
audienceLine._accentRangesNote: Word indices are 0-based, half-open [start, end). End is exclusive. AudienceLine.tsx reads these directly

privacy.heading: Your audio goes to Google. Then it is gone
privacy.subhead: Audio leaves your Mac once, for the transcription step. Google discards it when the call finishes. The transcript and the local audio file stay on your Mac until you delete them

cards.heading: Fast, accurate, free
cards.body: Audio uploads to Google's Gemini 2.5 Flash over HTTPS. Gemini transcribes it, returns a labelled transcript, and discards the audio. No retention. No training on your data. The endpoint is public: generativelanguage.googleapis.com

cards.heading: Your files, your Mac
cards.body: The transcript and the original audio live in ~/Library/Application Support/Corder/ until you delete them. Optional Dropbox archive uploads a copy to your own Dropbox app folder. It is off by default. Corder never uploads to any server other than Gemini for the transcription step

specList.value: Off by default, your Dropbox only
Remove the file from Finder, that is all

steps.heading: No bot in your call
steps.body: Corder records what your Mac plays and what your microphone hears at the OS level. The other side sees no third participant in the call

steps.heading: Studio-grade transcript, with playback
steps.body: Microphone and system audio go to Gemini as two parallel tracks, merged by timestamp. You get speaker columns and the original audio to scrub through later

steps.heading: Re-transcribe is free
steps.body: We cache raw turns by audio hash in a local SQLite. Coming back weeks later to re-map speakers or re-run the merge costs no extra API calls

steps.heading: No subscription. BYO Gemini key
steps.body: Bring your own Gemini API key. You pay Google about $0.30 per recorded hour at Flash rates, however many projects you're juggling

worksWith.heading: Records anything that plays through your Mac.
worksWith.subhead: Corder captures audio at the OS level. It works with any app that plays sound. The list below is where most people start.
worksWith.footnote: Records anything that plays through your Mac, including Teams meetings where bot-based recorders are being blocked from mid-2026

cells.heading: Per-speaker timeline
cells.body: A colour-coded bar per voice in the right pane. Click any tick to jump to that moment in the audio

cells.heading: Search the whole library
cells.body: Full-text search across every transcript you have ever made. Find any phrase, across any recording, from last month or this morning

cells.heading: Drag, don't export
cells.body: Drop a session into Notion, Obsidian, or Apple Notes. Text lands plain, without formatting clutter. No export dialog, no file picker, no CSV

cells.heading: Real Mac app, not a wrapper
cells.body: Cmd+W closes windows. The system Edit menu works. Sparkle delivers signed updates without the App Store. No Electron, no web runtime

cells.heading: Optional Dropbox archive
cells.body: Auto-upload audio and transcript to your own Dropbox app folder. Off by default. Enable it once from the menu bar; it runs quietly after that

cells.heading: Sparkle auto-updates
cells.body: Signed updates ship through the standard Mac mechanism. No App Store review delay, no manual downloads. You get the fix the day it ships

pricing.heading: One app, three ways
pricing.subhead: Every tier includes speaker labels, full-text search, drag-out to any app, Sparkle auto-updates, and optional Dropbox archive

tiers.name: Free
features: Drag-out to Notion, Obsidian, Apple Notes

tiers.name: Pro
features: Priority support, direct line to the maker
3 months free when you pay annually

tiers.name: Personal
features: No limit on individual recording length
3 months free when you pay annually

lifetime.name: Lifetime
lifetime.subline: Unlimited Gemini 2.5 Flash forever. Includes a 30-day Pro trial, then Flash free or $14/month to stay on Pro
features: All current and future Free tier features

pricing.microcopy: Pricing in USD. No credit card to download. Cancel anytime

items.q: Does Corder send my audio to the cloud?
items.a: Yes, for the transcription step. Audio uploads over HTTPS to Google's Gemini 2.5 Flash at generativelanguage.googleapis.com. Google transcribes it and discards the audio when the call finishes. The transcript and the local audio file stay on your Mac in ~/Library/Application Support/Corder/ until you delete them

items.q: Does the other side know I am recording?
items.a: Corder records system audio at the OS level. No third participant joins your call. The other side sees nothing different in their participant list and receives no recording-disclosure prompt from a SaaS. Whether you tell the other side you are recording is a legal and professional question that depends on your jurisdiction. Corder does not handle disclosure for you

On your Mac, in ~/Library/Application Support/Corder/. If you turn on Dropbox archive, a copy goes to your own Dropbox app folder. Delete the file from Finder to remove it. Corder has no server-side storage

items.q: Does it record Zoom, Meet, Discord?
items.a: Yes. Corder captures system audio at the OS level, so it works with anything that plays sound on your Mac: Zoom, Google Meet, Discord, Teams, FaceTime, or a room mic plugged into the audio interface. The platform does not matter

Gemini 2.5 Flash handles most major languages. English, Russian, and mixed-language calls work well. Other languages work too; accuracy depends on the model. If you need consistent accuracy on a specific language, test the free tier first

items.q: Does it need an internet connection?
items.a: For transcription, yes. Recording itself works offline. The audio files locally and the transcript fills in once you reconnect. If you close the app before reconnecting, transcription runs the next time you open it

items.q: Which version of macOS does it require?
items.a: macOS 14 Sonoma or later. Apple Silicon is recommended; Intel Macs work too

Select any part of the transcript and drag it into Notion, Obsidian, Apple Notes, or any text editor. Text lands plain. There is no export dialog, no file format to choose, no download button. If you want a plain text file, drag to the Finder

items.q: What is the difference between Flash and Pro?
items.a: Gemini 2.5 Flash is fast, accurate, and included in every tier. It handles most calls well. Gemini 2.5 Pro is a larger model with better accuracy on noisy rooms, cross-talk, heavy accents, and fast speakers. On the Pro tier, you choose the model before each recording

items.q: Is there a free trial for the Pro model?
items.a: The Free and Personal tiers use Flash only. The Lifetime tier includes a 30-day Pro trial. Pro subscribers access Gemini 2.5 Pro from day one. There is no separate trial for Pro outside of those paths

fit.heading: Is Corder for you?
fit.subhead: Two cards. Read the one that sounds like you

yes.heading: Yes, if you:
items: Discuss technical work on those meetings
Don't want a third-party bot in your call
Often come back to what was said in past meetings

no.heading: Probably not, if you:
items: Want live captions while the call is happening
Need team-shared transcripts or workspace permissions

comparison.heading: Why Corder
comparison.subhead: We don't name competitors on most of the page, but here's where Corder lands when you put us next to everyone

finalCta.lineUs: Free download. About $0.30 per recorded hour goes to Google
finalCta.footer: macOS 14+, Apple Silicon, Bring your own Gemini API key

footer.slogan: A meeting recorder that doesn't join the meeting
footer.madeIn: Made on a MacBook in Leicester, UK

newsletter.heading: Be the first to know
newsletter.subhead: Pro discounts, new feature drops, the occasional release note. No spam
newsletter.successMessage: Thanks. You'll hear from us when something ships

steps.heading: Start from the menu bar
steps.body: Open Corder from the macOS menu bar and hit Record. The app starts capturing system audio and your microphone at the OS level. No bot joins the call, the other side sees no third participant

steps.heading: Have your meeting
steps.body: Run your call on Zoom, Meet, Teams, Discord, anywhere. Corder records two parallel tracks, your microphone and everything that plays through your Mac. It works for as long as the meeting runs

steps.heading: Stop, read, drag
steps.body: Hit Stop. The transcript fills in with speaker labels. The audio file sits next to it on your Mac. Drag either one into Notion, Apple Notes, anywhere you keep your work
